title: The Slow Descent
intention: To travel gently through the body from head to feet, releasing what each region has been holding.

lines.text: Welcome. Let yourself arrive here, just as you are.
Settle into whatever holds you, and let it take your weight.
Close your eyes, or let them rest half-lit, soft as dusk.
Breathe in slowly. And let the breath leave without being pushed.
We will travel down through you, like light moving over a hillside at evening.
Begin at the crown of your head. Feel the quiet weight of it.
Let the scalp loosen, every small tension melting like frost in morning sun.
Soften the forehead. Let it become wide and smooth.
Unclench the jaw. Let the teeth part, the tongue grow heavy and still.
Around the eyes, let everything go dark and kind.
Now the neck. Feel it lengthen, a slow stem easing toward the earth.
Let the shoulders fall. Let them give up the day they have been carrying.
Breathe into them once more, and on the out-breath, let them drop a little further.
Travel down the arms now. The upper arms, the elbows, soft.
Down through the forearms, into the wrists.
And into the hands, where the fingers open like petals at rest.
Come back to the chest. Feel it rise and fall, unhurried.
Let the heart beat in its own quiet weather, asking nothing of you.
Soften the belly. Let it round and release with each breath.
Let the back sink, vertebra by vertebra, into the ground beneath you.
Breathe here a while. Let the whole middle of you grow warm and slow.
Now the hips. Let them widen, let them rest like stones settling in a riverbed.
Down through the thighs. Feel the long muscles unwind.
Let the knees release, and the weight pool low.
Into the calves now, soft as wet sand.
Down to the ankles, and into the feet.
Let the soles open, let the toes loosen and spread.
And now, for a moment, the whole of you at once.
From crown to feet, one quiet country, breathing.
Nothing to hold. Nothing to fix. Only this gentle weight.
Rest here, where the light has settled all the way down.
Let each breath rock you a little deeper into stillness.
When you are ready, there is nowhere to go. Simply stay.
Soften once more, and let the quiet keep you.
